narration: House Robber asks for the best safe total at each house position.
callout: At each spot, we compare skip-this-house with rob-this-house.

narration: If we rob the current house, we must jump back two houses before adding its money.
decision: For house 4, compare keeping 11 versus taking 1 plus the best answer from two houses back.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Why do we look two houses back when we rob the current house?

choices.label: Because neighboring houses cannot both be robbed
choices.feedback: Correct. Robbing this house blocks the one right before it.

choices.label: Because the houses are sorted
choices.feedback: Not this one. The rule is about neighbors, not sorting.

narration: This DP keeps only the best score so far at each position, not every robbery path.
callout: That is why DP is different from backtracking here.
